response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack web and application developer whose skills are almost entirely orthogonal to this role. The Principal Performance Architect position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, deep learning workload analysis, and GPU/SIMD architecture — none of which appear anywhere in the candidate's profile. His strongest technical differentiators are web frameworks, TypeScript/Node.js, GIS platforms, and low-latency HTTP microservices. The brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments and low-latency DLang work at Dunnhumby show systems-level awareness but fall far short of the hardware-software co-design and ML performance analysis this role requires.
strengths: Long career (14+ years) demonstrating sustained technical growth and adaptability across multiple domains
Low-latency systems experience at Dunnhumby (millions of req/s, async I/O, high-performance databases) shows some performance-oriented thinking
Hands-on C/C++ work at National Instruments provides a thin baseline of systems-level programming
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator hardware — the core technical requirements of the role
No background in deep learning, neural network training/inference optimization, or AI workload analysis of any kind
Seniority level (Senior SWE) and education (BS + ~14 years in web/app development) do not align with the Principal IC hardware-architecture track this role sits on
